decks.notes: The main focus of this deck is to quickly and aggressively deal damage to the opponent using low-cost spells and creatures. It utilizes cards like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage to put pressure on the opponent while also having access to removal spells like Fiery Impulse and Reckless Rage to deal with threats on the board. The deck also includes card draw and selection options to maintain momentum and consistency in its aggressive game plan. Overall, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with quick and efficient damage while maintaining a level of control over the battlefield.

The deck focuses on quickly overwhelming the opponent with aggressive creatures and burn spells, aiming to control the board and reduce the opponent's life total as fast as possible. It includes a mix of cheap creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage, along with burn spells like Fiery Impulse and Reckless Rage to remove threats and push damage through. The deck also includes card draw and versatility with cards like Consider and Stormchaser's Talent, allowing for adaptability in different situations. The aim is to maintain pressure and tempo throughout the game.

The deck focuses on creating a powerful combo with Greasefang, Okiba Boss and Parhelion II to overwhelm the opponent. It includes key cards like Esika's Chariot and Iron-Shield Elf to support the combo while also providing protection with cards like Bitter Triumph and Thoughtseize. The deck also includes various land cards to ensure consistent mana production and includes removal spells like Witherbloom Command to deal with threats. Overall, the deck aims to quickly assemble its combo pieces and dominate the game with powerful creatures and synergies.

This UR Aggro deck focuses on a fast-paced, aggressive strategy by utilizing low-cost spells and creatures to deal damage quickly to the opponent. By combining efficient creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage with burn spells like Fiery Impulse and Reckless Rage, the deck aims to apply constant pressure on the opponent's life total. The deck also includes card draw and mana fixing options to ensure a consistent flow of threats and answers. Overall, the deck prioritizes speed and aggression to overwhelm the opponent and secure a victory.

The deck focuses on using combo pieces such as Greasefang and Parhelion II to create a strong and resilient board presence. It also includes cards like Fatal Push and Thoughtseize for removal and disruption. The deck's mana base is built to support these combo pieces, with a mix of lands that provide colored mana and utility. Overall, the strategy is to assemble the combo pieces quickly while controlling the opponent's board to secure victory in a Pioneer 1 vs 1 format.

The key strategy of this Rakdos Aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures and disruptive spells. By playing powerful threats like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse and Bloodtithe Harvester, the deck aims to put pressure on the opponent early in the game. Support cards like Unholy Annex / Ritual Chamber and Thoughtseize help to control the opponent's resources and disrupt their game plan. Utilizing a mix of removal spells and discard effects, this deck aims to disrupt the opponent's strategy while maximizing its own aggression to secure a swift victory.

This deck focuses on low-cost creatures and powerful spells to overwhelm the opponent quickly. With strong aggro potential, the deck aims to establish board presence early with creatures like Archon of Emeria and Skyclave Apparition, supported by mana acceleration from Elvish Mystic and Llanowar Elves. Thalia, Guardian of Thraben disrupts the opponent's game plan while Elspeth, Storm Slayer and Collected Company provide additional threats and value. The efficient creatures and disruptive elements make it difficult for opponents to stabilize, allowing this Selesnya Aggro deck to win through fast and relentless aggression.

The deck focuses on quickly putting pressure on the opponent with cheap and efficient creatures, backed up by burn spells and pump effects to finish off the opponent quickly. It looks to control the board early with spells like Fiery Impulse and Reckless Rage, while maintaining a steady stream of creature and spell casts to keep up the pressure. The inclusion of Vivi Ornitier allows for card draw and additional burn damage, while cards like Consider and Stock Up help keep the deck running smoothly. Overall, the deck is designed to be fast and aggressive, overwhelming the opponent before they have a chance to recover.

This Rakdos Aggro deck focuses on aggressive creature strategies and disruption to control the board and pressure the opponent early on. By using efficient removal spells like Fatal Push and Thoughtseize, along with powerful threats like Bloodtithe Harvester and Sheoldred, the Apocalypse, the deck aims to quickly take control of the game and finish off the opponent before they can stabilize. The deck also includes some graveyard synergies with cards like Graveyard Trespasser and Kroxa, Titan of Death's Hunger to provide additional value and disruption options.

The deck revolves around assembling the Greasefang Parhelion combo, using cards like Fleeting Spirit, Monument to Endurance, and Parhelion II to generate value and control the game. Thoughtseize and Fatal Push are included for disruption, while Iron-Shield Elf and Guardian of New Benalia provide protection and support for the combo pieces. The deck also includes various lands to fix mana and provide utility, with Urborg, Tomb of Yawgmoth and Godless Shrine enabling black mana sources. Overall, the deck aims to create a powerful combo engine that overwhelms opponents with its synergy and versatility.

The main concept of this Selesnya Aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent with efficient creatures and disruptive spells. By using cards like Archon of Emeria, Enduring Innocence, Skyclave Apparition, and Thalia, Guardian of Thraben, the deck aims to slow down the opponent's game plan while simultaneously ramping up its own board presence with cards such as Llanowar Elves and Elvish Mystic. The deck also includes cards like Aven Interrupter and Collected Company to capitalize on its creature-heavy strategy and maintain pressure on the opponent.

The key concept of this UR Aggro deck is to use a combination of low-cost spells and creatures with prowess abilities to quickly apply pressure to the opponent and disrupt their game plan. By constantly adding to the board and attacking aggressively, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent before they can stabilize. This deck also utilizes cards like Academic Dispute and Consider to draw into more threats and answers, while cards like Boomerang Basics and Reckless Rage provide additional removal options to clear the way for attacks.

The deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's game plan through various removal spells and hand disruption cards, while also generating value through graveyard interactions and creature threats. It aims to control the pace of the game and outvalue the opponent by utilizing cards like Abrupt Decay, Fatal Push, Thoughtseize, and Sheoldred, the Apocalypse. The deck also has a mana base that supports its control strategy, with lands like Mutavault and Blooming Marsh providing versatile mana sources. Overall, the deck is designed to grind out advantages and eventually overwhelm the opponent with its powerful threats.

This Rakdos Aggro deck focuses on using aggressive creatures and efficient removal spells to overwhelm opponents quickly. It includes powerful cards like Bloodtithe Harvester and Sheoldred, the Apocalypse to apply pressure and disrupt the opponent's strategy. The deck also includes cards like Fatal Push and Thoughtseize to control the board and disrupt the opponent's hand. By combining aggressive creatures with disruptive spells, this deck aims to establish early board presence and maintain pressure throughout the game, ultimately aiming to outpace and outmuscle the opponent.

The main idea of this deck is to control the game through removal spells and disruptive cards, while also utilizing graveyard interactions to gain advantage. The deck aims to outvalue the opponent by consistently removing threats and disrupting their game plan, while also exploiting graveyard synergies with cards like Graveyard Trespasser and Sheoldred, the Apocalypse. This control archetype focuses on establishing a strong board presence and resource advantage to eventually overwhelm the opponent and secure victory in a longer, grindy game.

This aggressive red deck focuses on quickly overwhelming the opponent with low-cost creatures and powerful burn spells. It aims to deal damage early and often, utilizing cards like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage to build momentum. With efficient removal like Abrade and Lightning Strike, along with direct damage spells like Burst Lightning and Monstrous Rage, it aims to maintain pressure on the opponent. The deck's key strategy is to maintain a fast-paced tempo and close out the game before the opponent can stabilize.
